title: `podman run ... nixos/nix nix-build ...` fails with "error: renaming..." on kernel overlayfs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running a specific container command gives an error if using overlayfs. I've tried to find someone with knowledge about the application in question at https://discourse.nixos.org/t/weird-error-when-running-nixos-nix-under-podman/18056 before reporting this, but nobody stepped up.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

As a user with kernel overlayfs (which afaict is the default if no storage.conf is present)

```
> podman run --rm -ti nixos/nix nix-build -E \
    "builtins.fetchTarball { name = \"nixpkgs-src\"; url = \"https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/archive/59ee4cee790d077072dc1392e94fa6ac0099760d.tar.gz\"; sha256 = \"1k7qkpr7ay4qvkzmljl1kalpvj28ni36pmyy15nyfylrb18q3d9b\"; }"
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
error: renaming '/nix/store/add-1-2/x' to '/nix/store/9liwm7rnx4357qzgz3bk8v37nkbsj07z-nixpkgs-src'
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
error: expression does not evaluate to a derivation (or a set or list of those)
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Doesn't happen as root or with fuse-overlayfs.
I configured fuse-overlayfs by adding the following `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` and `podman system reset`:

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

I feel like `strace`ing could be helpful, but when I tried I wasn't able to spot anything.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 12:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

This is with fuse, here's the diff after removing storage.conf and running `podman system reset`:

```
--- with_fuse	2022-03-14 00:36:14.128811110 +0100
+++ without_fuse	2022-03-14 00:36:28.738978395 +0100

   kernel: 5.16.14-arch1-1
   linkmode: dynamic
   logDriver: journald
-  memFree: 10694381568

   ociRuntime:

       libseccomp: 2.5.3

-  uptime: 10m 28.02s
+  uptime: 10m 42.64s

   log:
   - k8s-file

-      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs

-      Version: |-
-        fusermount3 version: 3.10.5

-        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31

-    Native Overlay Diff: "false"

     Using metacopy: "false"

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

Current arch as of 2022-03-13
```
> pacman -Qi podman
Name            : podman
Version         : 4.0.1-2
Description     : Tool and library for running OCI-based containers in pods
Architecture    : x86_64

Optional Deps   : apparmor: for AppArmor support
                  btrfs-progs: support btrfs backend devices [installed]
                  catatonit: --init flag support
                  netavark: for a new container-network-stack implementation

Validated By    : Signature
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes, 4.0.1 is even more current than what the readme says D:

There's an entry about overlayfs but the fact that strace doesn't report the mknod system call makes me think it's not that:

```
> podman run --rm -ti --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE nixos/nix
bash-4.4# strace -e mknod nix-build -E "builtins.fetchTarball { name = \"nixpkgs-src\"; url = \"https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/archive/59ee4cee790d077072dc1392e94fa6ac0099760d.tar.gz\"; sha256 = \"1k7qkpr7ay4qvkzmljl1kalpvj28ni36pmyy15nyfylrb18q3d9b\"; }"
error: renaming '/nix/store/add-23-2/x' to '/nix/store/9liwm7rnx4357qzgz3bk8v37nkbsj07z-nixpkgs-src'
+++ exited with 1 +++
bash-4.4# strace -e mknod mknod bar c 1 3
mknod("bar", S_IFCHR|0666, makedev(0x1, 0x3)) = -1 EPERM (Operation not permitted)
mknod: bar: Operation not permitted
+++ exited with 1 +++
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Desktop PC on arch linux with nix installed, but I'm pretty confident my system nix doesn't interact with this in any way.

comments.body:
That is expected.  `mknod` is a privileged operation and cannot be used by unprivileged users, even in a user namespace.

`mknod` can be used only when running as root on the host.

I specifically addressed this

     strace **doesn't** report the mknod system call

@giuseppe please reopen this issue

Also, it works as a user when using fuse-overlayfs, so I'm pretty sure that's not it
sorry.  Do you see any `EXDEV` error coming from `rename`?

No worries, I know it's sometimes hard to triage issues.

Sadly, I'm not sure what nix does internally. I've created the thread on the nix discourse to debug this, but nobody could offer any insight. I'm tempted to create a matching issue in the nix github repo and cross-link this one.

`EXDEV` was a great hint. I looked through the 180MB strace log and found this:

```
rename("/nix/store/add-36-2/x", "/nix/store/9liwm7rnx4357qzgz3bk8v37nkbsj07z-nixpkgs-src") = -1 EXDEV (Invalid cross-device link)
```

I found [this patch](https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/stable/stable-queue.git/tree/queue-4.19/ovl-fix-missing-override-creds-in-link-of-a-metacopy-upper.patch?id=c245b0f13a29cb144a68bfef17b227229b4162ce) referenced by [this arch forum thread](https://bbs.archlinux.org/viewtopic.php?id=241866), but a) I'm on kernel 5.16.14-arch1-1 and this patch is ancient and b) it still happens after `echo N > /sys/module/overlay/parameters/metacopy`.
